summary: PromptLibrary currently binds prompts to DB-backed bots via a UUID chatbot_id only, so registry/code-defined agents (whose identity is a string agent_id) cannot have public prompts. The change has two pillars: (1) extend PromptLibrary to also accept agent_id (string), and teach PromptLibraryManagement to GET-filter by chatbot_id or agent_id; (2) introduce a new UserPrompts model + /api/v1/agents/user_prompts handler keyed by (user_id, chatbot_id), where chatbot_id is a free-form VARCHAR to accommodate both UUID strings and agent slugs.

localization.role: Model to alter (add agent_id, relax chatbot_id constraint)
Handler to teach about agent_id filtering on GET
Reference pattern for per-user model + composite PK
Reference DDL pattern (FK CASCADE, indexes, trigger, comments)
Existing route wiring; add UserPromptsManagement.configure here

constraints.statement: agent_id is always a Python str (registry slug) across bots/agents — never a UUID.
Per-user resources in this codebase use composite PK (user_id, <resource_pk>) with FK to auth.users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE.
DDL pattern for new models is a separate <model>_creation.sql file, NOT embedded in the docstring — PromptLibrary's embedded-DDL approach is the exception.
User identity inside handlers is obtained via `await self.get_userid(session=self._session)`.
Route wiring uses ModelView.configure(app, path) in app.py; the class-level `path` attribute is a fallback only.
PromptLibrary has zero existing test coverage; we should add at least a smoke test alongside the new model.

hypotheses.statement: PromptLibrary should gain an `agent_id VARCHAR NULL` column with a CHECK constraint enforcing that exactly one of (chatbot_id, agent_id) is set per row. The Python model relaxes `chatbot_id` to `Optional[uuid.UUID]` and adds `agent_id: Optional[str]`. PromptLibraryManagement adds GET filtering by either key.

UserPrompts is a new model at navigator.users_prompts, keyed by composite PK (user_id, prompt_id) with `chatbot_id VARCHAR` (free-form to support UUIDs OR agent slugs), FK to auth.users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE, plus UNIQUE (user_id, chatbot_id, title) to prevent duplicates per (user,bot). Exposed via a new UserPromptsManagement(ModelView) at /api/v1/agents/user_prompts.

Migration uses a single ALTER TABLE block for the existing prompt_library (add column, alter constraint) and a fresh CREATE TABLE for users_prompts. Both go into a new migration SQL inside the model file (for PromptLibrary) and into a new users_prompts_creation.sql (for UserPrompts).

confidence_map.claim: Single PromptLibrary model file + handler file
agent_id is always str across the bot/agent codebase
users_bots is the right reference for the new UserPrompts model
Route wiring path is in app.py, not the class attribute
Authenticated user_id is read via self.get_userid()
CHECK constraint on (chatbot_id XOR agent_id) is acceptable to the user
No existing tests for PromptLibrary — gap to fill optionally

unknowns.question: For the existing public PromptLibrary: should rows be uniquely identified by (chatbot_id|agent_id, title), or do we allow duplicate titles within the same bot/agent?
unknowns.user_answer: Yes — add UNIQUE constraint on (bot_or_agent, title).

unknowns.question: For UserPrompts: should `prompt_category` and `prompt_tags` mirror PromptLibrary, or be simplified for the per-user store?
unknowns.user_answer: Mirror — keep both prompt_category and prompt_tags for parity with the public library.

unknowns.question: For UserPrompts visibility/sharing: are these strictly private to user_id, or do we want a future `is_public BOOLEAN` flag (kept defaulted to FALSE)?
unknowns.user_answer: Yes — add is_public BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE so prompts can later be promoted to public.

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence (single model, single handler, single sibling pattern) and the change set is well-bounded. A spec can codify the migration, model fields, handler GET-filter behaviour, and the new endpoint without further architectural exploration.
